root_text.an1.21:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ abhāvitaṁ akammaniyaṁ hoti yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.22:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ bhāvitaṁ kammaniyaṁ hoti yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.23:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ abhāvitaṁ mahato anatthāya saṁvattati yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.23:1.2: Cittaṁ, bhikkhave, abhāvitaṁ mahato anatthāya saṁvattatī”ti.
root_text.an1.24:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ bhāvitaṁ mahato atthāya saṁvattati yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.24:1.2: Cittaṁ, bhikkhave, bhāvitaṁ mahato atthāya saṁvattatī”ti.
root_text.an1.25:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ abhāvitaṁ apātubhūtaṁ mahato anatthāya saṁvattati yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.25:1.2: Cittaṁ, bhikkhave, abhāvitaṁ apātubhūtaṁ mahato anatthāya saṁvattatī”ti.
root_text.an1.26:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ bhāvitaṁ pātubhūtaṁ mahato atthāya saṁvattati yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.26:1.2: Cittaṁ, bhikkhave, bhāvitaṁ pātubhūtaṁ mahato atthāya saṁvattatī”ti.
root_text.an1.27:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ abhāvitaṁ abahulīkataṁ mahato anatthāya saṁvattati yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.27:1.2: Cittaṁ, bhikkhave, abhāvitaṁ abahulīkataṁ mahato anatthāya saṁvattatī”ti.
root_text.an1.28:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ bhāvitaṁ bahulīkataṁ mahato atthāya saṁvattati yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.28:1.2: Cittaṁ, bhikkhave, bhāvitaṁ bahulīkataṁ mahato atthāya saṁvattatī”ti.
root_text.an1.29:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ abhāvitaṁ abahulīkataṁ dukkhādhivahaṁ hoti yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.29:1.2: Cittaṁ, bhikkhave, abhāvitaṁ abahulīkataṁ dukkhādhivahaṁ hotī”ti.
root_text.an1.30:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yaṁ evaṁ bhāvitaṁ bahulīkataṁ sukhādhivahaṁ hoti yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ.
root_text.an1.30:1.2: Cittaṁ, bhikkhave, bhāvitaṁ bahulīkataṁ sukhādhivahaṁ hotī”ti.

translation_text.an1.21:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it’s not developed like this, is as unworkable as the mind.
translation_text.an1.22:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it is developed like this, is as workable as the mind.
translation_text.an1.23:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it’s not developed like this, is so very harmful as the mind.
translation_text.an1.23:1.2: An undeveloped mind is very harmful.”
translation_text.an1.24:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it is developed like this, is so very beneficial as the mind.
translation_text.an1.24:1.2: A developed mind is very beneficial.”
translation_text.an1.25:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it’s not developed, with such untapped potential, is so very harmful as the mind.
translation_text.an1.25:1.2: An undeveloped mind, with untapped potential, is very harmful.”
translation_text.an1.26:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it is developed, with its potential realized, is so very beneficial as the mind.
translation_text.an1.26:1.2: A developed mind, with its potential realized, is very beneficial.”
translation_text.an1.27:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it’s not developed and cultivated, is so very harmful as the mind.
translation_text.an1.27:1.2: An undeveloped and uncultivated mind is very harmful.”
translation_text.an1.28:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it is developed and cultivated, is so very beneficial as the mind.
translation_text.an1.28:1.2: A developed and cultivated mind is very beneficial.”
translation_text.an1.29:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it’s not developed and cultivated, brings such suffering as the mind.
translation_text.an1.29:1.2: An undeveloped and uncultivated mind brings suffering.”
translation_text.an1.30:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that, when it is developed and cultivated, brings such happiness as the mind.
translation_text.an1.30:1.2: A developed and cultivated mind brings happiness.”

variant_text.an1.21:1.1: yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ → yathayidaṁ cittaṁ (bj, pts1ed)
